          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Generic, TypeVar\n\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\n\nfrom miniq.retry import RetryPolicy\n\nPayloadModelT = TypeVar(\"PayloadModelT\", bound=REDACTED TaskContext:\n    job_id: str\n    job_type: str\n    queue: str\n    attempt: int\n    logger: Any\n    is_cancel_requested: Callable[[], Awaitable[bool]]\n\n    async def sleep(self, seconds: float) -> None:\n        await asyncio.sleep(seconds)\n\n\nTaskHandler = Callable[[PayloadModelT, TaskContext], Awaitable[dict[str, Any]]]\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=REDACTED TaskDefinition(Generic[PayloadModelT]):\n    name: str\n    payload_model: type[PayloadModelT]\n    handler: TaskHandler[PayloadModelT]\n    retry_policy: RetryPolicy\n\n"

          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=REDACTED RetryPolicy:\n    base_delay_seconds: float\n    multiplier: float\n    cap_seconds: float\n    jitter_ratio: float\n\n\ndef calculate_retry_delay(*, job_id: str, attempt: int, policy: RetryPolicy) -> float:\n    if attempt < 1:\n        msg = \"attempt must be >= 1\"\n        raise ValueError(msg)\n    base_delay = min(policy.cap_seconds, policy.base_delay_seconds * (policy.multiplier ** (attempt - 1)))\n    digest = hashlib.sha256(f\"{job_id}:{attempt}\".encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()\n    fraction = int(digest[:8], 16) / 0xFFFFFFFF\n    min_factor = 1.0 - policy.jitter_ratio\n    max_factor = 1.0 + policy.jitter_ratio\n    factor = min_factor + ((max_factor - min_factor) * fraction)\n    delay = base_delay * factor\n    return min(policy.cap_seconds, max(0.0, delay))\n\n"
        },

          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom pydantic import Field\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\n\n\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=REDACTED\".env\", env_prefix=REDACTED\"MINIQ_\", extra=REDACTED\"ignore\")\n\n    service_name: str = \"miniq\"\n    api_host: str = \"0.0.0.0\"\n    api_port: int = 8000\n    worker_metrics_port: int = 9101\n    scheduler_metrics_port: int = 9102\n    redis_url: str = \"redis://redis:6379/0\"\n    redis_group: str = \"workers\"\n    default_queue: str = \"default\"\n    worker_queues: str = \"default\"\n    worker_concurrency: int = 4\n    worker_poll_block_ms: int = 1000\n    worker_visibility_timeout_ms: int = 10_000\n    worker_reclaim_interval_seconds: float = 2.0\n    scheduler_interval_seconds: float = 1.0\n    scheduler_config_path: Path = Path(\"config/schedules.yaml\")\n    db_path: Path = Path(\"/data/miniq.db\")\n    log_level: str = \"INFO\"\n    retry_base_delay_seconds: float = 1.0\n    retry_multiplier: float = 2.0\n    retry_cap_seconds: float = 30.0\n    retry_jitter_ratio: float = 0.2\n    health_timeout_seconds: float = 2.0\n    http_timeout_seconds: float = 5.0\n    api_base_url: str = Field(\"http://localhost:8000\", description=REDACTED\"Used by the CLI.\")\n    scheduler_batch_size: int = 128\n    broker_read_count: int = 8\n\n    @property\n    def database_url(self) -> str:\n        return f\"sqlite+aiosqlite:///{self.db_path.as_posix()}\"\n\n    @property\n    def queue_names(self) -> list[str]:\n        items = [item.strip() for item in self.worker_queues.split(\",\")]\n        return [item for item in items if item]\n\n\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\n    return Settings()\n\n"

          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom redis.asyncio import Redis\n\nfrom miniq.observability.metrics import jobs_enqueued_total\nfrom miniq.utils import utc_now\n\n\ndef stream_name(queue: str) -> str:\n    return f\"jobs:{queue}\"\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=REDACTED StreamMessage:\n    queue: str\n    stream: str\n    stream_id: str\n    values: dict[str, str]\n\n\nclass RedisBroker:\n    def __init__(self, redis_client: Redis, *, group_name: str) -> None:\n        self._redis = redis_client\n        self._group_name = group_name\n\n    async def ping(self) -> bool:\n        return bool(await self._redis.ping())\n\n    async def ensure_group(self, queue: str) -> None:\n        name = stream_name(queue)\n        try:\n            await self._redis.xgroup_create(name=REDACTED, groupname=REDACTED, id=REDACTED\"0\", mkstream=REDACTED        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001\n            if \"BUSYGROUP\" not in str(exc):\n                raise\n\n    async def enqueue(\n        self,\n        *,\n        queue: str,\n        job_id: str,\n        job_type: str,\n        enqueued_at: datetime | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        timestamp = (enqueued_at or utc_now()).isoformat()\n        message_id = await self._redis.xadd(\n            stream_name(queue),\n            {\n                \"job_id\": job_id,\n                \"queue\": queue,\n                \"type\": job_type,\n                \"enqueued_at\": timestamp,\n            },\n        )\n        jobs_enqueued_total.labels(type=REDACTED, queue=REDACTED        return message_id\n\n    async def publish_dlq(self, *, job_id: str, queue: str, job_type: str, reason: str) -> str:\n        return await self._redis.xadd(\n            \"jobs:dlq\",\n            {\n                \"job_id\": job_id,\n                \"queue\": queue,\n                \"type\": job_type,\n                \"reason\": reason,\n                \"published_at\": utc_now().isoformat(),\n            },\n        )\n\n    async def read_group(\n        self,\n        *,\n        queues: list[str],\n        consumer_name: str,\n        count: int,\n        block_ms: int,\n    ) -> list[StreamMessage]:\n        streams = {stream_name(queue): \">\" for queue in queues}\n        response = await self._redis.xreadgroup(\n            groupname=REDACTED,\n            consumername=REDACTED,\n            streams=REDACTED,\n            count=REDACTED,\n            block=REDACTED,\n        )\n        return self._normalize_messages(response)\n\n    async def reclaim(\n        self,\n        *,\n        queues: list[str],\n        consumer_name: str,\n        min_idle_time_ms: int,\n        count: int,\n    ) -> list[StreamMessage]:\n        reclaimed: list[StreamMessage] = []\n        for queue in queues:\n            name = stream_name(queue)\n            try:\n                response = await self._redis.xautoclaim(\n                    name=REDACTED,\n                    groupname=REDACTED,\n                    consumername=REDACTED,\n                    min_idle_time=REDACTED,\n                    start_id=REDACTED\"0-0\",\n                    count=REDACTED,\n                )\n            except TypeError:\n                pending = await self._redis.xpending_range(\n                    name,\n                    self._group_name,\n                    \"-\",\n                    \"+\",\n                    count,\n                    consumername=None,\n                )\n                ids = [item[\"message_id\"] for item in pending if item[\"idle\"] >= min_idle_time_ms]\n                if not ids:\n                    continue\n                claimed = await self._redis.xclaim(\n                    name,\n                    self._group_name,\n                    consumer_name,\n                    min_idle_time_ms,\n                    ids,\n                )\n                reclaimed.extend(\n                    self._normalize_messages(\n                        [\n                            (\n                                name,\n                                claimed,\n                            )\n                        ]\n                    )\n                )\n                continue\n            if isinstance(response, tuple) and len(response) >= 2:\n                messages = response[1]\n                reclaimed.extend(self._normalize_messages([(name, messages)]))\n        return reclaimed\n\n    async def ack(self, *, queue: str, stream_id: str) -> int:\n        return int(await self._redis.xack(stream_name(queue), self._group_name, stream_id))\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        await self._redis.aclose()\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _normalize_messages(response: Any) -> list[StreamMessage]:\n        normalized: list[StreamMessage] = []\n        for raw_stream, entries in response or []:\n            stream = raw_stream.decode() if isinstance(raw_stream, bytes) else raw_stream\n            queue = stream.split(\":\", 1)[1]\n            for raw_id, raw_values in entries:\n                stream_id = raw_id.decode() if isinstance(raw_id, bytes) else raw_id\n                values: dict[str, str] = {}\n                for key, value in raw_values.items():\n                    normalized[\n                        :\n                    ]  # no-op to keep mypy happy with list inference in nested loop\n                    parsed_key = key.decode() if isinstance(key, bytes) else str(key)\n                    parsed_value = value.decode() if isinstance(value, bytes) else str(value)\n                    values[parsed_key] = parsed_value\n                normalized.append(\n                    StreamMessage(\n                        queue=REDACTED,\n                        stream=REDACTED,\n                        stream_id=REDACTED,\n                        values=REDACTED,\n                    )\n                )\n        return normalized\n\n"

          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom enum import StrEnum\n\n\nclass JobState(StrEnum):\n    QUEUED = \"QUEUED\"\n    RUNNING = \"RUNNING\"\n    SUCCEEDED = \"SUCCEEDED\"\n    FAILED = \"FAILED\"\n    CANCELED = \"CANCELED\"\n    DEAD = \"DEAD\"\n\n\nTERMINAL_STATES = {\n    JobState.SUCCEEDED,\n    JobState.FAILED,\n    JobState.CANCELED,\n    JobState.DEAD,\n}\n\nACTIVE_STATES = {\n    JobState.QUEUED,\n    JobState.RUNNING,\n}\n\n"

          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom collections.abc import Iterable\n\nfrom prometheus_client import CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST, Counter, Gauge, Histogram, generate_latest\n\njobs_enqueued_total = Counter(\n    \"jobs_enqueued_total\",\n    \"Total jobs enqueued to Redis Streams.\",\n    labelnames=REDACTED\"type\", \"queue\"),\n)\njobs_started_total = Counter(\n    \"jobs_started_total\",\n    \"Total jobs started by workers.\",\n    labelnames=REDACTED\"type\",),\n)\njobs_finished_total = Counter(\n    \"jobs_finished_total\",\n    \"Total jobs finished by state.\",\n    labelnames=REDACTED\"type\", \"state\"),\n)\njob_duration_seconds = Histogram(\n    \"job_duration_seconds\",\n    \"Observed job execution duration in seconds.\",\n    labelnames=REDACTED\"type\",),\n    buckets=REDACTED, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 30, 60),\n)\nqueue_depth = Gauge(\n    \"queue_depth\",\n    \"Number of due queued jobs per queue.\",\n    labelnames=REDACTED\"queue\",),\n)\nworker_reclaims_total = Counter(\n    \"worker_reclaims_total\",\n    \"Number of Redis pending messages reclaimed by workers.\",\n)\nscheduler_enqueues_total = Counter(\n    \"scheduler_enqueues_total\",\n    \"Number of recurring schedule jobs created.\",\n    labelnames=REDACTED\"schedule_id\",),\n)\n\n\ndef set_queue_depths(depths: Iterable[tuple[str, int]]) -> None:\n    for queue_name, count in depths:\n        queue_depth.labels(queue=REDACTED render_metrics() -> tuple[bytes, str]:\n    return generate_latest(), CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST\n\n"
